skill.name: ckm-brand
skill.description: Create and maintain brand voice, visual identity, messaging frameworks, asset rules, color palettes, and design token guidance for AI-assisted brand work.
skill.summary: Build consistent brand guidance, assets, messaging, and design tokens for AI-assisted teams.
skill.author: nextlevelbuilder

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many high and critical patterns, but review found most are false positives from markdown examples, color hex values, regexes, and local file operations. The skill still warrants a medium warning because it includes local Node.js scripts, one shell-based execSync call, and prompt-context generation from user-controlled brand documents.

medium_findings.title: Shell Command Built With execSync
medium_findings.description: The token sync script imports child_process and runs a shell command string to regenerate CSS tokens. The target script path is derived from the current workspace path and is not shell-escaped, so unusual workspace paths could break command parsing or create injection risk.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: Direct evidence shows execSync receives a composed shell string. The command is for local token generation, so malicious intent is not confirmed, but shell execution risk is real.

medium_findings.title: User-Controlled Brand Files Become Prompt Context
medium_findings.description: The context injection script reads a brand guidelines file and emits selected content as prompt context. This is intended behavior, but a malicious or untrusted brand file could introduce instructions that influence later model responses.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The script clearly reads caller-selected content and prints prompt text. No malicious payload was found in the skill files, so the finding is a contextual risk rather than confirmed abuse.

low_findings.title: Broad Local File Reads Are Expected For Asset Tools
low_findings.description: Several scripts accept local paths and read files to validate assets, parse brand guidelines, or extract colors. This matches the skill purpose, but users should avoid running the scripts on sensitive files or untrusted workspaces.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Filesystem access is explicit and local. The behavior is legitimate for brand and asset utilities, with residual risk if users provide sensitive file paths.

low_findings.title: Static High-Severity Detections Are Mostly False Positives
low_findings.description: Weak cryptography and C2 keyword detections map to color hex values, markdown examples, regexes, and preset names rather than encryption, command-and-control, or obfuscated behavior. No prompt injection phrases were found during targeted review.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Reviewed examples show benign color presets, RGB parsing, and documented commands. The semantic context does not support the analyzer critical heuristic claims.

dangerous_patterns.title: Synchronous Shell Execution
dangerous_patterns.description: A local script uses execSync with a shell command string. Replace this with execFileSync or spawnSync and pass arguments as an array to avoid shell parsing.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The dangerous pattern is directly present. The command is local and conditional, which lowers confidence in exploitability.

dangerous_patterns.title: Generated Shell Commands Include User Paths
dangerous_patterns.description: The color extraction helper prints ImageMagick and Python commands that include the selected image path. These commands are instructions only, but users should inspect paths before copying them into a shell.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The script does not execute these generated commands. Risk depends on a user copying generated shell text from an untrusted path.

content.value_statement: Brand teams lose time when voice, visuals, assets, and design tokens drift across tools. This skill gives Claude, Codex, and Claude Code structured brand guidance, validation scripts, and reusable templates.
actual_capabilities: Creates brand voice, messaging, and visual identity guidance from structured references.
Provides a starter brand guidelines template for new or refreshed brands.
Extracts brand colors and prompt context from local markdown guidelines.
Validates asset names, sizes, formats, and manifest registration.
Syncs selected brand colors into design token JSON and CSS files.
Documents logo, typography, approval, consistency, and asset organization rules.
limitations: Does not verify legal trademark clearance or brand ownership rights.
Image color extraction requires external tools or another multimodal skill.
Script outputs depend on the local brand guideline format.
Users must review generated brand guidance before publishing.

use_cases.title: Launch a New Brand Kit
use_cases.description: Create voice, color, typography, logo, and messaging guidance before producing launch assets.

use_cases.title: Keep Marketing Assets Consistent
use_cases.description: Validate file naming, sizes, formats, and palette alignment before assets enter review.

use_cases.title: Sync Brand Decisions Into UI Tokens
use_cases.description: Convert approved brand colors from guidelines into design token files used by interfaces.

prompt_templates.title: Create Basic Brand Guidelines
prompt_templates.prompt: Use ckm-brand to create starter brand guidelines for [company]. Include voice, colors, typography, messaging, and asset rules.

prompt_templates.title: Review Brand Consistency
prompt_templates.prompt: Use ckm-brand to review these materials against our guidelines. Identify voice, color, typography, naming, and approval issues.

prompt_templates.title: Build Messaging Framework
prompt_templates.prompt: Use ckm-brand to create a messaging framework for [audience] and [offer]. Include positioning, proof points, and tone guidance.

prompt_templates.title: Sync Brand To Design Tokens
prompt_templates.prompt: Use ckm-brand update to refresh our primary, secondary, and accent colors. Then sync guidelines into design tokens and summarize changed files.

output_examples.input: Create brand guidance for a premium analytics product.
output_examples.output: A practical guide with brand personality, core attributes, color direction, typography choices, messaging pillars, and asset usage rules.

output_examples.input: Review this campaign asset list for brand compliance.
output: Filename issues are grouped by asset type.
Format and size warnings are listed separately.
Approval next steps are summarized for the team.

output_examples.input: Refresh the brand palette and sync it to design tokens.
output_examples.output: A concise update report naming the theme, selected colors, token files changed, and verification steps completed.

best_practices: Keep one approved brand guidelines file as the source of truth.
Review generated prompt context before using it in production workflows.
Run asset validation before design review or campaign handoff.
anti_patterns: Do not treat generated brand rules as legal trademark approval.
Do not run local scripts on sensitive files from untrusted workspaces.
Do not update design tokens without confirming the source brand colors.

faq.question: What does this skill help create?
faq.answer: It helps create brand voice, visual identity, messaging, asset rules, color palettes, and related design token guidance.

faq.question: Can it work with Claude, Codex, and Claude Code?
faq.answer: Yes. The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code workflows.

faq.question: Does it generate finished logo files?
faq.answer: No. It provides logo usage rules and brand guidance, but final artwork still needs design review.

faq.question: Does it make network requests?
faq.answer: No active network code was found during review. Some documentation includes example URLs or external tool references.

faq.question: Why is the security rating medium?
faq.answer: The skill includes local scripts, filesystem access, and one shell command used for token generation.

faq.question: How should teams use the scripts safely?
faq.answer: Run them only in trusted workspaces, inspect generated shell commands, and review prompt context before reuse.
